title: Serialize editor.getJSON() into typed blocks and persist
description: Implement the mapping from Tiptap's `editor.getJSON()` document output to the `blocks` table rows (one row per top-level node), plus the reverse mapping (blocks → Tiptap document) used when loading a tab for editing.
acceptanceCriteria: A pure function `tiptapDocToBlocks(doc: JSONContent, tabId: string): BlockInsert[]` maps each top-level node in the Tiptap document to a `blocks` row shape (`tipo`, `conteudo_json`, `ordem` by position)
A pure function `blocksToTiptapDoc(blocks: Block[]): JSONContent` reconstructs a valid Tiptap document from stored blocks, ordered by `ordem`
Round-tripping a document through `tiptapDocToBlocks` then `blocksToTiptapDoc` produces a document that renders identically in the editor (no data loss for any of the 8 block types)
`blocks.saveDraft` (protectedProcedure mutation, see TASK-32) uses `tiptapDocToBlocks` to persist
Loading a tab's content editor route uses `blocksToTiptapDoc` (via a `blocks.getByTab` query) to initialize the editor

steps.details: In `packages/schema/src/serialize.ts` (or `apps/server/src/blocks/serialize.ts` if it needs no client usage), map `doc.content` array entries to block rows, using each node's `type` as `tipo` and the full node as `conteudo_json`, with `ordem` set by array index.
Implement the inverse: sort blocks by `ordem`, parse each `conteudo_json`, and assemble `{ type: 'doc', content: [...] }`.
Add `blocks: { getByTab: protectedProcedure.input(z.object({ tabId: z.string() })).query(...) }` to a new `apps/server/src/trpc/routers/blocks.ts`, returning blocks ordered by `ordem`.

steps.description: Wire ContentEditor to load and initialize from blocks
steps.details: In `apps/admin/src/features/editor/ContentEditor.tsx`, call `blocks.getByTab`, convert to a Tiptap doc via `blocksToTiptapDoc`, and pass it as the editor's initial `content`.

steps.description: Write round-trip tests for every block type
steps.details: Write a test that constructs a sample Tiptap document containing one node of each of the 8 MVP block types, runs it through `tiptapDocToBlocks` then `blocksToTiptapDoc`, and asserts the result deep-equals the original document.

technicalNotes: This task is the load-bearing bridge between the editor's runtime document model and the persisted `blocks` table — get the round-trip test passing for all 8 types before building autosave/publish on top of it.
